ogTitle: Lort River vs Lower Chittering: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lort River and Lower Chittering.
heading: Lort River vs Lower Chittering — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lort River, Western Australia and Lower Chittering, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lort River has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Lower Chittering (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lort River or Lower Chittering safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lort River has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Lower Chittering scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lort River scores 15/100 for bushfire risk. Lower Chittering scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lort River or Lower Chittering?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lort River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
